skill.name: context-degradation
skill.description: Diagnoses long-context failures in AI agents, including lost-in-middle behavior, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and conflicting context.
skill.summary: Identify context degradation patterns and choose practical mitigation strategies for AI agents.
skill.author: ChakshuGautam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, documentation terms, regex examples, and local analysis utilities with no network, shell execution, credential access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown fenced examples in documentation. They do not execute commands or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence markers or documentation examples. No shell, subprocess, eval, or external command API appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Documentation
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain context engineering prose, examples, or local scoring logic. No cryptographic algorithm, hash primitive, or security-sensitive crypto use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no md5, sha1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation. The matches are ordinary context degradation text and example logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Used as Text Markers
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings refer to source-related language inside documentation and hallucination marker lists. The script does not inspect the host system or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged script line is a phrase used to detect uncertain claims, and the SKILL.md line discusses multi-source retrieval. No OS, network, process, or account reconnaissance behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Long AI conversations can lose key details, mix tasks, or amplify mistakes. This skill helps diagnose those failures and choose practical mitigation strategies.
actual_capabilities: Explains lost-in-middle, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and context clash patterns.
Provides symptoms for diagnosing long conversation and agent performance failures.
Recommends mitigation strategies such as compaction, filtering, segmentation, and isolation.
Includes a Python helper for local context health scoring and recommendation generation.
Supplies reference patterns for attention distribution, relevance scoring, and recovery planning.
limitations: The included attention calculations are simulations, not direct model internals.
The Python helper uses simple heuristics and needs adaptation for production monitoring.
Model threshold examples may become outdated as model behavior changes.
The skill diagnoses context issues but does not automatically modify an agent system.

use_cases.title: Debug agent failures
use_cases.description: Identify whether a long-running agent forgot constraints, followed stale context, or mixed unrelated task requirements.

use_cases.title: Design reliable long-context workflows
use_cases.description: Plan context placement, retrieval filtering, and compaction rules before deploying agents with large prompts.

use_cases.title: Evaluate context architecture
use_cases.description: Compare context management choices against known degradation patterns and recovery strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an agent starts giving lower quality answers in a long session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this conversation for context degradation. Identify likely patterns, cite symptoms, and recommend the first three fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Lost-in-middle review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when important requirements may be buried in a large prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this context layout for lost-in-middle risk. Tell me which information should move to the beginning or end.

prompt_templates.title: Poisoning and clash analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when corrections do not stick or sources conflict.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this agent trace for context poisoning and context clash. Separate confirmed conflicts from uncertain evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture mitigation plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when designing a production agent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a context degradation mitigation plan for this agent. Include filtering, compaction triggers, isolation boundaries, and evaluation checks.

output_examples.input: An agent ignored requirements from the middle of a long planning prompt.
output_examples.output: The likely issue is lost-in-middle degradation. Move critical constraints to the opening task block and repeat final acceptance criteria near the end.

output_examples.input: A support bot keeps repeating a wrong account status after being corrected.
output_examples.output: This suggests context poisoning. Remove the incorrect status from active context, preserve only verified facts, and restart with a clean summary.

output_examples.input: A coding agent applies instructions from a previous feature to the current task.
output_examples.output: This matches context confusion. Segment task contexts, mark transitions clearly, and avoid carrying unrelated implementation notes forward.

best_practices: Keep critical goals and constraints near attention-favored positions.
Filter retrieved documents before adding them to active context.
Trigger compaction before context size causes visible quality loss.
anti_patterns: Adding every available document to context without relevance filtering.
Relying on one long session for unrelated tasks with different goals.
Treating generated summaries as verified facts without checking them.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps diagnose and reduce quality loss caused by long or conflicting AI context.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other long-context AI tools.

faq.question: Does the script call external services?
faq.answer: No. The included Python helper performs local heuristic analysis and does not make network requests.

faq.question: Can it prove exact attention weights?
faq.answer: No. The reference script simulates attention patterns and should be replaced with model-specific instrumentation when available.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when agent quality drops during long sessions, corrections fail, or context from different tasks appears mixed.

faq.question: Is it a production monitoring system?
faq.answer: No. It provides concepts, examples, and starter heuristics that teams can adapt for production evaluation.
